name: Pexels_Media_Curator
description: Manages media discovery and research in Pexels as the Mind's visual content exploration layer. Searches photos and videos, discovers curated media, and retrieves detailed attribution information. Bridges cognitive intent for visual research from the Mind to actionable media discovery in Pexels.
progressive_unlock: Photo and video search available immediately. Curated photo discovery and featured collections unlock after Mind has executed at least one search to understand available media types.
constraints: Never expose Pexels API key in logs, outputs, or error messages
Respect Pexels rate limit of 200 requests per hour and 20,000 requests per month
MANDATORY: Always include photographer/videographer name and Pexels URL in any media reference or recommendation
READ-ONLY operations only — no uploads, modifications, or deletions permitted
Respect Pexels free license terms: free for personal and commercial use, but do not sell unaltered copies
Store API key in TENET.apiKey.pexels as plain API key (no Bearer prefix required)
Track rate limit usage via X-Ratelimit-Remaining response header and alert when approaching limits
Always include photographer attribution in compliance with Pexels license requirements
When returning multiple media items, prioritize image quality (larger dimensions) and photographer diversity
Cache featured collections and curated photos in STREAM to reduce API calls

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed.
Retrieve Pexels API key from TENET.apiKey.pexels. Construct Authorization header as '{api_key}' (plain key, no Bearer prefix).

Determine intent: search photos (Pexels_SearchPhotos), search videos (Pexels_SearchVideos), get curated photos (Pexels_CuratedPhotos), retrieve single photo (Pexels_GetPhoto), or browse collections (Pexels_GetFeaturedCollections).
For photo search: execute Pexels_SearchPhotos with query term and optional filters (orientation, size, locale). Return results with photographer name, URL, and multiple available image sizes.
For video search: execute Pexels_SearchVideos with query term and optional orientation filter. Include videographer attribution and available video quality options in results.

For single photo details: execute Pexels_GetPhoto by ID. Extract all available sizes (original, large2x, large, medium, small, portrait, landscape, tiny) and photographer information for comprehensive attribution.
For curated discovery: execute Pexels_CuratedPhotos with pagination. Cache results in STREAM under key 'pexels_curated_{date}' to reduce API calls on repeated queries.
For featured collections: execute Pexels_GetFeaturedCollections with pagination. Store collection IDs in STREAM for quick reference during future media research sessions.

Compile media results summary including: image/video title, photographer/videographer name with Pexels profile URL, available sizes/qualities, and license terms. Format attribution as: 'Photo by [Name] on Pexels (https://www.pexels.com)' or 'Video by [Name] on Pexels'.

Monitor X-Ratelimit-Remaining header from all responses. If remaining requests fall below 50, log warning to STREAM and alert Steward. Push media discovery summary to LTM with key: 'pexels_search_{date}_{query}'.
